programme.desc: After the Allied landing in Normandy, France falls city after city. An island fortress off the coast becomes the stage for an act of Nazi defiance. Its commanders vow to hold it to the last man.

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: Deep in the forests of East Prussia, a sprawling Nazi complex becomes a nerve centre; one of the most secure facilities in the Nazi empire, how does it become the site of a plot against the Führer?

programme.title: The Nature of Things
programme.desc: Science is revealing the truth about "dad bods" and how having a child can dramatically affect the brains and bodies of men; real dad bods are less about the pudge and more about the smarts.

programme.title: The Nature of Things
programme.desc: Deep in the jungle amidst the ruins of an ancient empire, bat scientists meet for an all out Bat-a-thon, a blitz of research to discover more about the furry little flyers.

programme.title: The Nature of Things
programme.desc: Grasslands account for half of the total land area on the planet and most have been converted for human use; there are some places though where people and wildlife can both prosper by sharing space.

programme.desc: Twenty years after walking away from his quest for rock'n'roll glory, a mysterious tunesmith emerges from his parents' basement with a power-pop masterpiece.

programme.title: The Island Diaries
programme.desc: Despite its isolation, the people of the Falkland Islands enjoy a quality of life.

programme.title: Rik Mayall: Magnificent B'stard
programme.desc: A documentary tribute to the influential and much-missed comedian, a man driven by punk spirit and an unrelenting commitment to pushing boundaries. When he died in 2014 at the age of 56, Rik left behind a body of ground-breaking work, from Rick the Poet and Kevin Turvey to The Young Ones, Bottom and The New Statesman - performances that redefined television comedy and helped ignite the alternative

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of American film director Don Siegel, best known for 1956 sci-fi classic Invasion of the Body Snatchers and his work with Clint Eastwood, including Dirty Harry.

programme.title: Discovering: Richard Dreyfuss
programme.desc: A glimpse into the life and career of the Oscar-winning Hollywood actor who starred in The Goodbye Girl, Jaws and Down and Out in Beverly Hills.

programme.title: The Sixties
programme.desc: A look at the changing social landscape of America in the 1960s, from growing environmental concerns to civil rights and the women's movement.

programme.title: Discovering: Basil Rathbone
programme.desc: A profile of the English star of stage and screen, who appeared as the villainous Guy of Gisborne in The Adventures of Robin Hood and played Sherlock Holmes in 14 films.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of American film-maker and choreographer director Stanley Donen, best known for co-directing On the Town and Singin' in the Rain with Gene Kelly.

programme.title: Last Call: When a Serial Killer Stalked Queer New York
programme.desc: A fourth murder leads to the creation of a multi-jurisdictional task force and the first national press coverage. An eyewitness provides a name and description of the suspect.

programme.title: Rolling Stone: Stories from the Edge
programme.desc: A look at the magazine's move to New York, the impact of the death of Elvis Presley, and Hunter S Thompson's coverage of the Pulitzer divorce case.

programme.title: Lockerbie
programme.desc: A shock verdict leaves the families unsatisfied, but doubt is cast on the court's decision and 34 years after the bombing, a new suspect is taken into custody.

programme.title: Spitfire
programme.desc: The story of the British single-seat fighter plane the Supermarine Spitfire, designed by RJ Mitchell and famous for its role in the Battle of Britain. Archive footage, new film of the aircraft in flight and interviews with the last surviving veterans who flew the plane in combat combine to tell its story.

programme.title: When We Were Kings
programme.desc: Leon Gast's Oscar-winning documentary charting the challenge of an ageing Muhammad Ali for George Foreman's World Heavyweight title held in Zaire in 1974. The film explores the impact of Ali's visit to Africa before focusing on the epic fight, dubbed the 'Rumble in the Jungle', which has gone down in history as one of the greatest of all time. Spike Lee and Norman Mailer are among the contributors

programme.title: My Icon: Duke McKenzie
programme.desc: The former world champion boxer talks about the individuals that have been an inspiration to him over the course of his career.

programme.desc: A profile of the English star of stage and screen, who appeared as the villainous Guy of Gisborne in The Adventures of Robin Hood and played Sherlock Holmes in 14 films.

programme.title: UFO
programme.desc: The world reels from the New York Times bombshell, and some begin to wonder if the government may be using the alien narrative to cover up something more sinister.

programme.title: I'm Chevy Chase and You're Not
programme.desc: Chevy Chase was a cast member on the inaugural series of Saturday Night Live and he built on that success to star in numerous Hollywood comedies and host the Academy Awards. He played hapless father Clark Griswold in the National Lampoon films and ran amok in pictures including Caddyshack, Spies Like Us, Fletch and Three Amigos. Two-time Emmy Award winner Marina Zenovich directs an authorised feat

programme.desc: The life story of comedy star John Belushi, who captured the hearts and funny-bones of audiences worldwide before his tragic and untimely death aged 33.

programme.title: 2000 Meters to Andriivka
programme.desc: Documentary following a Ukrainian platoon as they traverse a heavily fortified mile of forest to liberate a strategic village from Russian forces.

programme.title: Kings of Coke
programme.desc: How a gang of Irish bank robbers from Montreal rose to become one of North America's most feared criminal organisations.

programme.desc: A profile of the English star of stage and screen, who appeared as the villainous Guy of Gisborne in The Adventures of Robin Hood and played Sherlock Holmes in 14 films.

programme.title: The Sixties
programme.desc: A look at America's changing morality during the decade, from attitudes towards sex and the use of drugs to a newfound expressiveness through music.

programme.title: Discovering: Jon Voight
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the actor, who starred in Midnight Cowboy, Deliverance and Runaway Train and won an Academy Award for his role in 1978 film Coming Home.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of George Stevens, who directed films including Shane, The Greatest Story Ever Told and The Diary of Anne Frank and won Oscars for A Place in the Sun and Giant.

programme.title: Last Call: When a Serial Killer Stalked Queer New York
programme.desc: A break in forensic technology leads investigators to a suspect. The next step is to decipher his past, going back almost 30 years to a murder in Maine.

programme.title: Rolling Stone: Stories from the Edge
programme.desc: A look at the magazine's explosive 1993 interview with Bill Clinton, its embracing of hip-hop, and the 2008 cover story Britney Spears: Inside an American Tragedy.

programme.title: Macaque Island
programme.desc: Exploring the lives of two troops of long-tailed macaques residing in the forests of Singapore, revealing the fascinating world of these curious and intelligent primates. Matnoor rules over the 30 members of the Street troop, wihile Aarav is the old alpha male of the wilder Hill troop.

programme.title: Tales from Zambia
programme.desc: A look at how the hot Luangwa River Valley, the dry Liuwa Plain and the cool Kasanka National Park offer very different habitats for Zambia's incredible wildlife.

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: Six-month-old Alejandra falls from a tree and badly damages one of her eyes. Clara and her one-year-old daughter Clarita are chosen for release back into the wild, but a flash flood threatens the mission. Meanwhile, the vets provide around-the-clock care for Erik, who has a dangerous infection, and attempt to reintroduce Dilla to her daughter Delilah.

programme.title: Arctic Secrets
programme.desc: Winter has arrived at the lower western shore of Hudson Bay, where polar bears reside. However, the ice that normally forms there is nowhere to be seen.

programme.title: Hope for Wildlife
programme.desc: A fisherman's illegal catch provides a feast for Hope's patients, and an invasive species also winds up on the menu.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: A pair of Mueller's gibbons develop a taste for life in the treetops, while three endangered golden-cheeked gibbons move into the enclosure next door. Meanwhile, a specialist doctor is called in when Freddy's bowel complaint fails to improve.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Alison Cronin visits Dao Tien island in Vietnam, the site of Monkey World's foreign sanctuary for endangered primates. Meanwhile, the team learns that two rare monkeys are being kept in a theme park outside Ho Chi Minh City, and tries to get them freed.

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: Sun bear cubs Denny Bear and India arrive at a sister sanctuary. A quiet day in the clinic turns hectic when two orangutans fall dangerously ill.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
